contents: Funds earned and spent before opening a dedicated business account should be classified according to their origination. For example, if your business received income, where did that money go? If you took the money personally, it would be considered either a 'distribution' or a 'loan' to you. On the flip side, if your business had an expense that you paid personally, that would be considered either a 'contribution of capital' or a 'loan' from you. Maintaining detailed records of all financial activities during the initial pre-incorporation phase is crucial for establishing a clear audit trail. Using a dedicated tracking method, even if it's a simple spreadsheet or separate notebook, helps distinguish these early personal funds from later business transactions.

Options trading involves specialized vocabulary like calls, puts, and strike prices. These terms form the foundation for more complex strategies traders employ. When you ""buy to open"", you are purchasing an option and opening a new position. When you ""sell to open"", you are creating a brand new options contract and selling it.  ""Covered"" means that you have assets in your account to satisfy the terms of the options contract. Theta quantifies the daily erosion of an option's extrinsic value as expiration approaches. Option traders analyze this Greek to anticipate how time decay may influence their portfolio's profitability over the holding period. Theta is the measure of the change in value in one day. So for every day that passes, the calls you sold are going down by $64.71 (which is positive to you since you sold them at a higher value) and the calls you sold are going down by $49.04. So an option position with high theta (a short spread) is gaining $15.67 each day (assuming no change in stock price or volatility).

Leveraging occurs when the value of your position is more than the value of what you are trading in. One example of this is borrowing money (i.e. creating debt for yourself) to buy bonds. The amount of your own funds that you are using to pay for the position is ""leveraged"" by the debt so that you are risking more than 100% of your capital if, for example, the bond became worthless).
